title: Module system with pv-te hybrid design for solar panel
abstract: The present invention relates to a module system used for solar panels with PV-TE (Photovoltaic - Thermoelectric) hybrid design for cooling the systems under proper conditions, thereby increasing the efficiency thereof in order to ensure the optimal electricity generation of PV (photovoltaic) systems. The present invention particularly relates to a module system that is capable of preventing potential interruptions in electricity generation by cleaning surfaces of PV panels from accumulated snow by being mounted thereon, and that is further capable of automatically detecting the presence of snowfall by means of a sensor integrated into a microcontroller board.
claims:
Claims 1.A module system used for solar panels with Photovoltaic- Thermoelectric hybrid structure, characterized by comprising; â¢ a module (100) comprising of a module frame (110) having a plurality of thermoelectric coolers (120); thermoelectric cooler (120) that allows for producing electric energy by receiving the waste heat generated by the photovoltaic (PV) solar panel (200), for cooling down the solar panel (200), and for melting the snow by heating the solar panel (200) when there is snow accumulation on the solar panel (200); and a module outer frame (130) having a conductive aluminum profile (131) that enables heat transfer by coming into contact with the solar panel (200) directly, â¢ a microcontroller board (140) that regularly receives data regarding the thickness of snow accumulated on the solar panel (200) from a snow detection sensor (150), that issues a command to the thermoelectric cooler (120) to heat the solar panel (200) when the thickness of the snow reaches to a user-defined value, that performs a wireless data exchange with the snow detection sensor (150) that further issues a command to said thermoelectric cooler (120) to cool down the solar panel (200) based on the data received by the snow detection sensor (150) indicating the thickness of snow when the snow melts completely, and â¢ a snow detection sensor (150) that optically measures the thickness of snow accumulated on the solar panel (200), that transmits snow thickness data to the microcontroller board (140) either continually or when 

 the thickness of snow accumulated on the solar panel (200) reaches to a user-defined value, 2.Module system according to Claim 1, characterized by comprising; thermoelectric cooler (120) that consists of cooler aluminum profile (121), thermal tape (122), thermoelectric Peltier (123), thermal paste (124) and rubber plate (125). 3.Module system according to Claim 1 or Claim 2, characterized by comprising; cooler aluminum profile (121) that prevents the thermoelectric Peltier (123) from overheating due to the air coming from outside, and that ensures airflow and the necessary ventilation by means of the channeled structure thereof. 4.Module system according to Claim 1 or Claim 2, characterized by comprising; thermal tape (122) that has a structure with high heat transfer coefficient, that prevents any direct contact between the cooler aluminum profile (121) and the thermoelectric Peltier (123), and that ensures the heat transfer therebetween. 5. Module system according to Claim 1 or Claim 2, characterized by comprising; thermoelectric Peltier (123) that allows for generating electricity by serving as a DC source by means of temperature differences between two surfaces thereof that enable the conversion between electrical energy and thermal energy. 6.Module system according to Claim 1 or Claim 2, characterized by comprising; thermal paste (124) that has a structure with high heat transfer coefficient, that prevents any direct contact between the thermoelectric Peltier (123) and the conductive aluminum profile (131), and that ensures the heat transfer therebetween. 7.Module system according to Claim 1 or Claim 2, characterized by comprising; rubber plate (125) which ensures that the inert heat received from the solar panel (200) is absorbed by the thermoelectric Peltier (123) and that the heat generated by the thermoelectric Peltier (123) is dissipated to the solar panel (200). 8.Module system according to Claim 1, characterized by comprising; a wired connection that enables the data flow between the detection sensor (150) and the microcontroller board (140).
